title: Retrieval Review: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences
summary: Retrieval Review: Accessible CSS under Real Preferences retrieves prior skills in an original changed zoom input and system-preference constraints scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css zoom reflow, css visible hidden content, css focus visibility, css user preferences, css touch targets, css accessibility regression in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed zoom input and system-preference constraints artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Volunteer Shift Deck accessibility hardening here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the accessible CSS regression system here. */

options.text: Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 2: opacity zero removes an element from keyboard and accessibility navigation.

Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 2: state visual, layout, focus, hit-test, and accessibility intent first, then choose hidden, display, visibility, opacity, or a tested visually hidden pattern.
Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 2: use visibility hidden for skip links and reveal them only after activation.

options.text: Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 7: a desktop layout can remain fixed because browser zoom simply scales the screenshot.

Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 7: test complete workflows at high zoom and narrow effective widths, then remove fixed dimensions, clipping, and nonwrapping assumptions that lose content.
Changed zoom input and system-preference constraints case 7: disable browser zoom with viewport metadata so the desktop layout remains stable.
